rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Avoid using package-qualified names in AGREE contracts that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report type errors, trace the root cause to an unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration and resolve it by referencing valid AADL features or declared helpers.
Ensure AGREE expressions use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and align helper declaration types with their assigned expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Binary expressions in AGREE must have boolean types on both sides of the '=>' operator.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types in AGREE contracts that are not already defined in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by adding architecture; instead, resolve the root cause of unresolved references or invalid declarations.
